# Dhaatrik Chowdhury — Full Site Content for LLM Indexing

> This is the extended version of llms.txt containing detailed content summaries for each page and blog post on dhaatrik.github.io. This file is intended for LLM crawlers and AI agents seeking comprehensive context about the site's content. **Site version: v4.0.1.**

---

## About the Author

**Dhaatrik Chowdhury** is an Entrepreneur and AI Engineer based in India. He has approximately 9 years of experience tutoring students in physics and mathematics across various formats — from school classrooms of 30–40 students, to offline coaching centers, one-on-one mentoring, and online sessions for NRI and international students. He has guided roughly 200 students over this period.

He is also known by his nickname **"Dhaatu"**, and his students affectionately call him **"Dhaatu Bhaiyaa"** (Hindi for "Big Brother Dhaatu"). This name became the basis for his YouTube channel, **DBS Classes** (Dhaatu Bhaiyaa's Classes).

### Core Philosophy
> "Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE" — and must be shared with "ZERO TERMS AND CONDITIONS"

### Professional Focus Areas
- Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning engineering
- Full-stack web development (React, TypeScript, Python, TensorFlow)
- Physics simulation and orbital mechanics
- Educational content creation and pedagogy

### Social Profiles
- GitHub: https://github.com/dhaatrik
- LinkedIn: https://linkedin.com/in/dhaatrik
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/dhaatrik
- Medium: https://medium.com/@dhaatrik

---

## Pages

### Home (https://dhaatrik.github.io/)
The landing page features a dynamic greeting based on time of day, a hero section introducing Dhaatrik as an Entrepreneur & AI Engineer, a showcase of 4 featured projects, a tech stack marquee, and the 3 most recent blog posts.

### Personnel (https://dhaatrik.github.io/personnel/)
A detailed personnel file covering Dhaatrik's background, education, work experience, and technical capabilities. Styled as a classified dossier with a technical/military aesthetic.

### Projects (https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/)
A dedicated archive displaying Dhaatrik Chowdhury's complete engineering directory. Displays card previews for all 12 projects.

Each project card includes:
- **IMPACT.LOG**: A 1–5 neon segment bar (`role="meter"`, `aria-label="Impact level: N out of 5"`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`) showing development difficulty/pain level.
- **[ FUCKUP LOG ]**: An honest one-sentence teaser describing the biggest mistake or failure in the project.
- **SYS.STATUS telemetry**: A live-animated monospace status line (e.g., `STATUS: V4_IN_DEV // DB: OFF_LINE_FIRST // SYNC: LOCAL`) with a pulsing `telemetry-keyword` class.
- **Mission tags**: Tech stack and domain tags.

### Pedagogy (https://dhaatrik.github.io/pedagogy/)
Dedicated to Dhaatrik's teaching philosophy and the DBS Classes YouTube channel. Contains:
- The founding story and core philosophy
- Teaching methodology (sibling-centric model, kitchen physics, outdoor experiments)
- A video archive grid of all 20 DBS Classes YouTube videos with subject filtering
- An honest reflection on the channel's cessation due to inconsistency

### Transmissions (https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/)
The blog index page listing all published articles ("transmissions") with reading times, dates, and tags.

Features a terminal-style search widget implementing the **WAI-ARIA 1.2 Combobox pattern** (no-popup listbox variant):
- Input: `role="combobox"`, `aria-autocomplete="list"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-activedescendant`
- Results container: `role="listbox"`  
- Each result card: `role="option"`, unique `id="search-result-N"`, `aria-selected`
- Keyboard nav: `/` to focus, ArrowDown to navigate results, ArrowUp to return to input, Escape to clear
- Supports multi-term fuzzy matching (space-delimited) and `#tag` shorthand
- Highlights matched text in result card titles and tags

---

## Blog Posts (Full Summaries)

### DeltaV Lab Mission Log: RK4, Web Workers, and the KSP Problem
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/deltav-lab-mission-log/
**Published:** 2026-06-17
**Tags:** aerospace, deltav-lab

First-person engineering mission log on building DeltaV Lab — a browser-based orbital mechanics simulator motivated by Kerbal Space Program's intuitive teaching and the need for mathematically rigorous validation. Covers design constraints (browser-only, deterministic integration, responsive UI), why RK4 was chosen over Euler integration, the 50Hz physics tick contract, Web Worker threading architecture separating UI from physics loop, Vehicle Assembly Building (VAB) design, and stack choices (TypeScript, Vanilla DOM, esbuild, Vitest). Includes Tsiolkovsky rocket equation and validation against analytical Keplerian solutions. Over 1100 words.

### Vellor Mission Log: Building an Offline OS for Tutors
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/vellor-power-tutor-log/
**Published:** 2026-06-17
**Tags:** pedagogy, vellor

First-person mission log on Vellor v4.0.0 "Power-Tutor" — a free offline-first PWA for independent tutors. Written after 9 years of tutoring experience witnessing overpriced, invasive tutoring software. Architecture: 100% client-side IndexedDB storage, Zustand persistence, zero server dependency, O(N) filter/sort operations. Covers v4 operational features (roster management, attendance tracking, gamified UI), honest tradeoffs (multi-device sync, onboarding friction, feature scope), and connection to DBS Classes philosophy ("Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE").

### Teaching From First Principles: A Mission Log on How I Actually Teach
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/teaching-from-first-principles/
**Published:** 2026-06-17
**Tags:** pedagogy

Comprehensive first-person teaching philosophy transmission. Core axiom: knowledge must be free with zero terms. Methodology pipeline: Observe → Question → Model → Formalize (equations last). Covers sibling-centric "Dhaatu Bhaiyaa" trust model, context-rich teaching (kitchens, streets, open skies), attention engineering via narrative lesson structure, code-switching across Class 9 through university levels, and honest acknowledgment of YouTube channel failure as a lesson in systems over motivation.

### Why I Started DBS Classes: The Physics of Curiosity
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/why-i-started-dbs-classes/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy, youtube

First-person origin story. After 9 years tutoring ~200 students across classrooms, coaching centers, and online groups, I founded DBS Classes on October 31, 2022 to combat rote memorization. Three curriculum pillars: intuitive physics/math, first-principles thinking, and bridging equations to real observations (bus wheels, cricket ball curve, football kinematics). Produced 20 video lessons on dimensional analysis, trigonometry, and kinematics. Core philosophy: "Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE" with zero terms and conditions.

### How I Teach: Bus Wheels, Kitchen Labs, and Open Skies
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/my-ways-of-teaching/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy

First-person mission log on teaching methodology, led by the bus wheel observation that sparked kinematics intuition on a city street. Kitchen demonstrations teach thermodynamics (Leidenfrost effect, conduction/convection). Outdoor classrooms for trigonometry and optics. Streets as mechanics labs for angular momentum, torque, and rolling resistance. Includes Pedagogy Pipeline: Observation → Conceptualization → Intuitive Map → Mathematics. Covers sibling-centric "Dhaatu Bhaiyaa" relationship model, attention engineering, and adaptive feedback across student levels.

### Why I Stopped Uploading: An Honest Audit of Consistency and Flaws
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/why-i-stopped-youtube-classes/
**Published:** 2026-06-16
**Tags:** pedagogy, youtube

First-person post-mortem. Final upload "Point Mass is a Myth" on September 15, 2023 became the tombstone of active production after 20 videos. Root causes: (1) solo production bottleneck and novelty fade, (2) illusion of "next week" compounding into years, (3) relying on motivation instead of systems. Includes Motivation vs. Systems comparison table. Lessons applied to engineering: reduce friction, lower cognitive load, sustainable pace. Channel remains as archive and reminder of structural discipline.

### Interactive Mission Logs: Orbital Insertion Dynamics
**URL:** https://dhaatrik.github.io/transmissions/scrollytelling-demo/
**Published:** 2026-05-29
**Tags:** aerospace, physics, ui-ux, deltav-lab

First-person scrollytelling demo born from Kerbal Space Program intuition and DeltaV Lab engineering. Explains why launch profiles need sequential visualization (Max Q, MECO, staging, circularization). Interactive Scrollytell component with three flight phases and telemetry graphics. Mathematical appendix covers Tsiolkovsky rocket equation with MDX-escaped KaTeX ($I_{sp}$, $v_{orbit}$). Argues scroll-driven pacing beats video for thinking through orbital insertion problems.

---

## Projects

### DeltaV Lab
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/deltav-lab/
- **Description**: Real-time orbital mechanics simulation engine using RK4 integration at 50Hz. Simulates gravitational dynamics, orbital transfers, and spacecraft trajectories.

### Vellor
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/vellor/
- **Description**: Offline-first note-taking application with local database synchronization. Currently in V4 development.

### FuelDrop
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/fueldrop/
- **Description**: High-concurrency fuel delivery platform. Version 3.0.0 shipped and operational.

### The Infinite Intelligence
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/the-infinite-intelligence/
- **Description**: Multi-agent AI debate system where multiple AI agents engage in structured parallel debates.

### Instant App Opener
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/instant-app-opener/
- **Description**: Electron-based local desktop launcher built with JavaScript, Node.js, and CSS. Summons productivity utilities via system-tray global keyboard listeners under 150ms.

### Glassbox
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/glassbox/
- **Description**: Secure Node.js proxy execution sandbox built with TypeScript, Node.js, VM2, and ES6 Proxies. Intercepts core system APIs to audit untrusted scripts locally.

### FinTrack
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/expense-vs-investment-tracker/
- **Description**: Offline-first personal finance tracker visualizing expense-vs-investment ratios using Chart.js, HTML/CSS, JavaScript, and localStorage.

### Seamless QR Dining
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/seamless-qr-dining/
- **Description**: Real-time restaurant menu, ordering, and billing pipeline utilizing WebSockets, Node.js, React, and Tailwind CSS.

### Vireo Wellness
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/vireo-wellness/
- **Description**: Client-side health tracker built with HTML/CSS, Vanilla JS, and IndexedDB, ensuring 100% privacy and local-only data persistence.

### Bill Express
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/bill-express/
- **Description**: Local PDF billing invoice generator utilizing client-side print layout and JavaScript to format print grids and produce downloadable PDFs instantly.

### MarkPDF
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/free-markdown-to-pdf-converter/
- **Description**: Markdown-to-PDF document compiler featuring Remark markdown parsing, CSS formatting, and KaTeX mathematical rendering in the browser.

### Read Aloud Chrome Extension
- **URL**: https://dhaatrik.github.io/projects/readaloud_chromeextension/
- **Description**: Manifest V3 Chrome extension wrapping the Web Speech API for custom text-to-speech with speech speed control and shortcut bindings.

---

## v4.0.0 Feature Additions

### Design System
- **Canonical `.glass-surface` base class**: All glassmorphic surfaces (bento cards, search bar, popover dialogs) now consume a single token system (`--glass-bg-raw`, `--glass-border-raw`, `--neon-glow-1-rgb`, `--neon-glow-2-rgb`). No component duplicates `rgba()` values — all update atomically when the theme changes.
- **`--glass-border-opacity` increased**: Light-mode rest state 0.08 → 0.12, hover 0.2 → 0.25 to meet WCAG 1.4.11 non-text contrast on white backgrounds.

### Scroll Reveal System
- **`data-reveal-delay` attribute**: Any `.reveal-on-scroll` element can declare `data-reveal-delay="N"` (milliseconds) for declarative per-element staggering without a `.reveal-stagger` parent.
- **`.blueprint-reveal` applied to hero parallax SVG**: The grid-background fades in on load, reinforcing mission-control aesthetics.
- **Hero CTAs migrated**: The `animate-fade-in-up + delay-{N}` system is replaced with the unified `reveal-on-scroll + data-reveal-delay` approach, removing a parallel animation system from the codebase.

### Accessibility (v4.0.0)
- **Terminal search combobox**: Full WAI-ARIA 1.2 combobox pattern (role="combobox", role="listbox", role="option", aria-expanded, aria-activedescendant).
- **IMPACT.LOG meter**: pain_level bar carries `role="meter"`, `aria-label="Impact level: N out of 5"`, `aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`.
- **Identity rotator**: `aria-live="off"` prevents screen readers from announcing every rotation.

### Footer Diagnostics (v4.0.0)
- `CURRENT_STUDY`: Hand-updated constant. Current topic: soil chemistry & crop physiology from first principles.
- `LATEST_INSIGHT`: Dynamically sourced from `posts[0].data.description` — auto-updates with every new transmission.
- `PAGES.INDEXED`: Auto-computed as `posts.length + projects.length + 4`.
- `SYS.LOAD`: From `navigator.hardwareConcurrency` (CPU core count).
- `SYS.LATENCY`: From `PerformanceNavigationTiming.responseStart - requestStart`.

### Reading List (v4.0.0)
- Added: *The Design of Everyday Things* (Don Norman) — systems thinking applied to UX decisions.
- Added: *Thinking, Fast and Slow* (Daniel Kahneman) — marked `IN_PROGRESS`.
- All existing book hints rewritten as first-person personal reactions, not back-cover descriptions.

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Who is Dhaatrik Chowdhury?**
A: Dhaatrik Chowdhury is an Entrepreneur and AI Engineer from India with 9 years of teaching experience in physics and mathematics and a focus on building AI-driven software solutions.

**Q: What is DBS Classes?**
A: DBS Classes stands for "Dhaatu Bhaiyaa's Classes" — a free YouTube channel for physics and mathematics education founded on October 31, 2022 by Dhaatrik Chowdhury. It produced 20 educational videos before ceasing uploads on September 15, 2023.

**Q: What is Dhaatrik's teaching philosophy?**
A: Dhaatrik believes in first-principles teaching where students learn through observation, intuition, and real-world experimentation rather than rote memorization. His core axiom is: "Education Is Not Free BUT KNOWLEDGE IS FREE" — shared with zero terms and conditions.

**Q: Why did Dhaatrik stop uploading on YouTube?**
A: Dhaatrik stopped due to internal execution failures: solo production bottleneck, loss of focus from other projects, and relying on motivation rather than building systematic production workflows. He has publicly documented this as a personal failure and learning experience.

---

## Agent / coding-agent instructions

Repository entry points: `AGENTS.md` (routing), `GEMINI.md` (SEO/AEO/GEO summary → `dhaatrik-seo-legacy` skill). Project skills live in `.agents/skills/`:

- **dhaatrik-writing-style** — voice and tone for all user-facing copy
- **dhaatrik-mission-report** — Mission Report structure for projects and transmissions
- **dhaatrik-astro-site** — Astro 6 architecture, components, tests, deployment
- **dhaatrik-visual-system** — sci-fi mission-control UI and design tokens
- **dhaatrik-seo-legacy** — honest SEO, JSON-LD, OG, sitemap/RSS

Index: `.agents/README.md`
